phrases.cultural_note: Czech directions often use landmarks like 'druhý skleník' (second greenhouse) instead of precise distances.

likely_response.local: Rovně a pak druhý skleník vlevo.
likely_response.translation_en: Straight ahead and then the second greenhouse on the left.

responses.english: Straight ahead and then the second greenhouse on the left.
responses.french: Rovně a pak druhý skleník vlevo.

phrases.text_local: Platí se dnes jen v hotovosti?
phrases.cultural_note: Smaller attractions in Czechia may have card terminals but disable them on weekends due to fees or staffing.

likely_response.local: Ano, o víkendu bereme jen hotovost.
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, on weekends we only take cash.

responses.english: Yes, on weekends we only take cash.
responses.french: Ano, o víkendu bereme jen hotovost.

phrases.cultural_note: Plant shops in Czech botanical gardens are commonly placed near the entrance/exit for convenience.

likely_response.translation_en: Next to the main gate, by the exit.

responses.english: Next to the main gate, by the exit.

phrases.intent_en: Can I buy annual pass here?
phrases.text_local: Mohu si tady koupit roční vstupenku?
phrases.cultural_note: Staff typically handle annual pass sales at the main entrance pokladna (ticket office).

likely_response.local: Ano, vystavím vám ji u pokladny.
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, I can issue it for you at the ticket office.

responses.english: Yes, I can issue it for you at the ticket office.
responses.french: Ano, vystavím vám ji u pokladny.

phrases.intent_en: How do I get to Japanese garden?
phrases.text_local: Jak se dostanu do japonské zahrady?
phrases.cultural_note: Using the informal name 'alpín' for the Alpine garden is common in casual spoken Czech.

likely_response.translation_en: Past the Alpine garden on the right, by the pond.

responses.english: Past the Alpine garden on the right, by the pond.
